data.title: Humana to support reforms for Medicare Advantage billing practices: WSJ
data.description:
Humana (NYSE:HUM), the second-largest Medicare Advantage insurer behind UnitedHealth (UNH), has informed congressional staffers it plans to back measures that would curb billing practices generating billions of dollars in additional payments to the industry, according to The Wall Street Journal.

According to a one-page policy overview and congressional staffers, Humana (NYSE:HUM) has informed congressional staff members that the company is prepared to back curbs on extra payments that insurers receive from diagnoses recorded through home visits conducted by nurse practitioners.

The Louisville, Kentucky-based managed care firm has also suggested reforms to the Medicare insurers’ practice of reviewing patients’ medical charts to arrive at diagnoses.

The company has said that the practice, known as Health Risk Assessments through chart reviews, shouldn’t lead to payments for diagnoses based on chart reviews without any specific medical encounters.

UnitedHealth (UNH) and CVS Health (CVS) operate HouseCalls and Signify Health, units focused on conducting HRAs through visits to their Medicare enrollees’ homes. Other insurers with large  Medicare Advantage businesses include Cigna (CI) and Alignment Healthcare (ALHC).

In October, the Office of Inspector General accused UnitedHealth (UNH), Humana (NYSE:HUM), and other Medicare firms of collecting as much as $7.5B in payments in 2023 by conducting annual HRAs without any follow-up.

HRAs conducted through home visits made up the largest portion of those extra payments, the OIG’s report highlighted.

data.title: Jacobs wins contract to deliver services for expansion of Boeing's St. Louis facility
data.description:
Jacobs (NYSE:J) +1.1% pre-market Thursday after saying it was selected to deliver design, engineering and environmental services for Boeing's (BA) multi-billion expansion at its St. Louis, Missouri, campus; financial terms were not disclosed.

The 1.1M sq ft expansion is designed to nearly double Boeing's (BA) regional manufacturing footprint, and will support advanced assembly facilities and the associated post assembly operations center for future generations of advanced aircraft, the companies said, adding that site construction is underway and will be completed in multiple phases during 2026-30.

"The largest investments Boeing is making in defense is in St. Louis," Boeing (BA) executive Mark Webb said, adding the site will continue to grow in facilities and headcount for the next several years.

"The new classified facilities will feature the greatest advancements in digital design and manufacturing the world has seen," Webb said.

data.title: Trump Media files initial registration statement for bitcoin ETF
data.description:
Social media platform operator Trump Media and Technology Group (NASDAQ:DJT) has filed an initial registration statement for an ETF that holds bitcoin directly and offers its shares to investors.

The company has filed the statement on Form S-1 of the Securities and Exchange Commission for the Truth Social Bitcoin ETF.

The ETF, expected to launch later this year, aims to reflect bitcoin’s price performance.

data.title: La Rosa Holdings establishes subsidiary in Madrid as part of Spanish expansion drive
data.description:
Real estate company La Rosa Holdings (NASDAQ:LRHC) announced on Thursday the establishment of a subsidiary in Madrid as part of the company's expansion plans in Spain.

Shares were +3.92% pre-market to $0.12.

The unit is currently in the process of obtaining local licenses for its operations.

With a view to developing the La Rosa Realty España brand, the company has entered into a letter of intent with the Spanish real estate professional, Keyla Torres Velasquez.

LRHC intends to convert Velasquez's two independently owned offices in Valencia and the Canary Islands into La Rosa-branded locations.

data.title: Vizsla raises stake in Mexican silver-gold project, secures $40M bought deal financing
data.description:
Vizsla Royalties (NYSE:VZLA) +9.3% pre-market Thursday after saying it agreed to acquire an additional 3.0% net smelter returns royalty on certain concessions of the Panuco-Copala silver-gold project in Mexico, increasing its NSR from 0.5% to 3.5% on Silverstone concessions and maintaining a 2% NSR on Rio Panuco concessions.

Under the deal terms, Vizsla (NYSE:VZLA) will exercise its right to repurchase 50% of the 3.0% royalty for $1.95M and purchase the remaining 50% of the 3.0% royalty for $38.05M, for a combined $40M in cash.

To fund the NSR acquisition, the company said it entered into an agreement for underwriter CIBC Capital Markets to purchase on a bought deal basis 27.4M common shares at C$2/share for C$54.8M in total gross proceeds, plus an underwriters option to purchase up to an additional 15% of the shares sold under the offering.

"We have now fully consolidated all royalties in the Panuco district, significantly increasing shareholder exposure to one of the highest-grade silver-gold districts in the world," Vizsla (VZLA) CEO Michael Pettingell said.

data.title: ZJK Industrial ramps up production to support Nvidia’s B40 project
data.description:
• ZJK Industrial (NASDAQ:ZJK) on Thursday announced that it is ramping up production to meet growing demand from Nvidia’s B40 project.
  • Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) is advancing the development of a customized AI accelerator, the B40 chip, specifically for the Chinese market. 
  • Mass production of the B40, which is based on Nvidia’s Blackwell architecture and targets the mid-to-high-end market segment, could be started as early as June 2025.
  • “As a supplier to Nvidia, we see tremendous growth potential in this project, given the market size and surging demand in the AI sector. We expect this initiative to contribute significant year-over-year revenue growth," Ning Ding, CEO of ZJK said.

data.title: Stock index futures rise sharply as traders digest labor reports
data.description:
Stock index futures rose sharply on Thursday, as investors assessed the latest labor data.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.6%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.33%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 2 basis points to 3.86%.

The initial jobless claims rose more than expected in the past week. 

Productivity growth was revised lower in the final Q1 estimates, and labor costs extended their climb.

The U.S. services sector leads in May job cuts, according to the Challenger Report.

Wall Street on Wednesday closed little changed, while bonds rallied after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years.

"Markets put in a strong performance yesterday, after a weak batch of US data led to a massive rally for U.S.Treasuries, which in turn supported risk assets," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Of course, even with the rally, the data still raised fears that the U.S. economy lost steam into May. That started off with the ADP’s report of private payrolls, which was the softest in over two years, Allen added. 

The main event in terms of labor data—the monthly employment reading—will land on Friday.

data.title: Vince McMahon sells 1.6M TKO shares for $250M - filing
data.description:
According to a Securities and Exchange filing disclosed by TKO Group (NYSE:TKO) on Thursday, Endeavor Operating Company (EOC) has purchased 1,579,080 Class A shares from Vince McMahon at $158.32 per share for a total transaction value of $250M.

The transaction concluded on June 4, 2025. EOC used available working capital to fund the purchases, the SEC filing shows.

EOC now indirectly owns 4,158,517 shares of TKO Group. 

SEC filing
